title: Initializing Weights
concept: Applying He or Xavier initialization to the model's weights to start training stably.

exercises.prompt: What is the function of activation layers like ReLU in a neural network?
exercises.explain: Activation layers introduce non-linearity to the model, allowing it to learn complex patterns.

cards.title: What is Weight Initialization?
cards.body: Weight initialization is the process of setting initial values for the parameters (weights) in a model. It impacts how well and how quickly the model can learn.

cards.title: He Initialization
cards.body: He initialization is a method of setting weights that helps prevent issues with gradients when using ReLU activation functions. It uses a specific formula based on the number of inputs to a layer.

cards.title: Think of Setting Up a Machine
cards.body: Imagine starting a machine with its parts properly aligned. If the parts (weights) are set correctly, it runs smoothly. He and Xavier initialization help 'align' the weights.

cards.title: Example of He Initialization
cards.body: Suppose a layer has 10 inputs; using He initialization, weights are set to small random values scaled by the square root of 2 divided by the number of inputs.

cards.title: Myth: Any Random Weights are Fine
cards.body: It's a myth that it doesn't matter how you initialize weights. Poor initialization can lead to slow learning or failing to learn altogether.

cards.title: Weight Initialization Comparison
cards.body: Here's how He and Xavier initialization differ in set up:

right.text: Balanced for sigmoid or tanh functions.

exercises.prompt: What is the main benefit of using He initialization?

options.text: It randomly initializes weights with no pattern.
It helps maintain a stable gradient for ReLU activations.
It makes the model's architecture simpler.
It speeds up the coding process without any effect.

exercises.explain: He initialization maintains a stable gradient, which is crucial for networks that use ReLU activation.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Xavier initialization is better suited for networks using ReLU activation functions.
exercises.explain: Xavier initialization is better for sigmoid or tanh activations, whereas He is preferred for ReLU.

exercises.prompt: Complete the sentence: He initialization helps in maintaining a _____ gradient for ReLU activations.
exercises.sentence: He initialization helps in maintaining a stable gradient for ReLU activations.
exercises.explain: He initialization focuses on keeping the gradient stable to ensure effective learning.

exercises.prompt: Which initialization method should be used for a network using ReLU activations?
exercises.explain: He initialization is specifically designed for networks using ReLU activations to prevent gradient issues.

exercises.prompt: When should you use He initialization instead of Xavier initialization?

options.text: Use He for sigmoid and Xavier for ReLU
Use He for networks with ReLU activations

exercises.explain: You should use He initialization for networks with ReLU activations because it helps maintain stable gradients, essential for effective learning.
